title: Improve large lookup tables
body:
I have a design which relies on large lookup tables/ROMs coded as very large Verilog constants. This eventually results in V3Premit inserting huge temporaries, and initializing them with inine code every time their use is reached:

https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/8624ce6a84dcd0908a9d721420e1f790ffaf4ba1/src/V3Premit.cpp#L169-L187

This results in hundreds of machine instructions being spent on constant initialzing a stack variable on every cycle, all so we can do 2 indexed lookups into the temp. This is both slow at runtime and at compile time as it bloats the function sizes in a way that output split cannot chop up.

An impovement would be to emit these constants as static data. I can have a go at this and V3Table has some helpful analogous code. What I am struggling with, is that I see all static arrays emitted in EmitC are inialized with code in _ctor_var_reset, which is still a bit wasteful and also racy (though probably harmless) with multiple simulation context. Is there a reason why we don't just emit these as C static data initializer as in:
```
static int foo[] = { 1, 2, 3,4 .... };
```
I appreciate this only works for primitive data types, so some mechanism is needed for referencig these in eg VlWide types.
